heading: Immediate relative and special immigrant visas
label: 1204 Immediate relative and special immigrant visas
text: A consular officer may, subject to the limitations provided in , issue an immigrant visa to a special immigrant or immediate relative as such upon satisfactory proof, under regulations prescribed under this chapter, that the applicant is entitled to special immigrant or immediate relative status.section 1201 of this title
notes: References in TextThis chapter, referred to in text, was in the original, “this Act”, meaning , , known as the Immigration and Nationality Act, which is classified principally to this chapter. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.act June 27, 1952, ch. 47766 Stat. 163section 1101 of this title
Amendments1965— struck out reference to sections 1154 and 1155 of this title and substituted “special immigrant or immediate relative” for “nonquota immigrant”.Pub. L. 89–236
